summary: Move large runtime-control stage outputs from SQLite inline payloads to file-backed artifacts.
rationale: Runtime-control stage outputs can exceed the size that should live inline in SQLite rows. This change keeps SQLite as the control plane, adds artifact refs for large payloads, and makes get/list callers resolve artifacts transparently while retention and privacy erasure delete the files with the database records.
deletion_targets: large stage output JSON stored inline in runtime_control_stage_outputs
artifact files left outside retention cleanup
artifact files left outside privacy erasure cleanup
line_count_rationale: store.py is already a pre-existing oversized runtime-control persistence module. This PR touches it surgically to keep artifact refs, transactions, retention, and transparent output resolution in the same control-plane implementation instead of splitting one storage invariant across a new abstraction.
risks: Artifact refs and SQLite rows must commit or roll back together so failed writes do not orphan files.
Retention must not delete database rows while leaving payload files behind.
Privacy erasure must remove both inline candidate traces and file-backed stage output artifacts.
